_comment: SPY ACCUMULATION strategy. Goal: slowly grow a core SPY position over the years while buying low and selling high. Each row in 'strategies' is one approach the backtest runs and compares (against a plain 'buy weekly / DCA' benchmark). 'defaults' apply to every strategy unless the row overrides them. Growth comes from buying DOLLAR chunks (fractional shares, rounded to 6 decimals like Robinhood) and only trimming a FRACTION of the OPPORTUNISTIC sleeve -- baseline buys are a protected core that is never sold.

_param_help.signal: How to judge low vs high: 'weekly' (this week's move vs a normal week) or 'trend' (price vs its 200-day average).
_param_help.base_buy_dollars: Steady buy every week no matter what (the accumulation core). 0 = pure timing, no steady buy.
_param_help.buy_dollars: EXTRA dollars to buy each time SPY is 'low' (on top of any baseline).
_param_help.trim_pct: Fraction of the OPPORTUNISTIC sleeve to sell when 'high'. The baseline core is never sold.
_param_help.entry_z: How far below normal counts as 'low enough to buy extra' (standard deviations).
_param_help.sell_z: How far above normal counts as 'high enough to trim'.
_param_help.cooldown_days: Min trading days between opportunistic buys/trims (5 ~= once a week). The baseline buy ignores this.

_bot_allocation._comment: Whole-bot capital split + total bankroll (source of truth for live/paper sizing). The paper brains (live_spy.py, live.py) read this via allocation.py and size their orders to each sleeve. 'total_capital' is YOUR number to edit -- it's a simulated/paper bankroll for now; raise it (or top up) as you add money. Backtest math ignores this block.
